extensionDescription.message: Integrate Jira Cloud and Jira Server into Thunderbird

optionsCloudTab.description: Tab label for Jira Cloud connection

optionsServerTab.description: Tab label for Jira Server connection

labelJiraUrl.description: Label for the Jira instance URL field

labelApiToken.description: Label for the API token field (Cloud)

placeholderApiToken.description: Placeholder for the API token field

buttonTestConnection.description: Label for the test connection button

testSuccess.description: Message shown when the connection test succeeds

testFailure.description: Message shown when the connection test fails

errorNotConfigured.message: Jira is not configured. Open ThunderJira options page to set up the connection.
errorNotConfigured.description: Error when Jira config is missing

errorPermissionDenied.message: Permission denied. The extension needs access to this URL to connect to Jira.
errorPermissionDenied.description: Error when the user denies the host permission request

savedSuccessfully.description: Message shown after settings are saved

uiSectionTitle.description: Title of the interface/UI preferences section in options

labelShowOptionalFields.description: Label for the show optional fields checkbox

labelShowOptionalFieldsDesc.message: Optional fields in the create issue form will be expanded by default.
labelShowOptionalFieldsDesc.description: Description shown below the show optional fields checkbox

labelLoadRemoteContent.message: Load remote content in issue popup
labelLoadRemoteContent.description: Label for the load remote content checkbox

labelLoadRemoteContentDesc.message: When enabled, avatars and icons are loaded from Jira when expanding an issue link. Disable to show text-only previews.
labelLoadRemoteContentDesc.description: Description shown below the load remote content checkbox

debugSectionTitle.description: Title of the developer/debug section in options

labelDebugMode.description: Label for the debug mode checkbox

labelDebugModeDesc.message: Prints detailed logs to the browser console (DevTools). Disable when not needed.
labelDebugModeDesc.description: Description shown below the debug mode checkbox

linkReleaseNotes.description: Link/button to open the release notes page

privacyNoticeTitle.message: Data sent to your Jira server
privacyNoticeTitle.description: Title of the privacy notice section at the bottom of the options page

privacyNoticeText.message: ThunderJira sends data only to the Jira instance you configured above. No telemetry is collected and no third-party servers are contacted. The following information is transmitted to your Jira server: your authentication credentials (email and API token for Cloud, or Personal Access Token for Server/Data Center) on every request; the email content you choose to use (subject, body and headers such as From, To, Cc and Date) when creating an issue or adding a comment; the values you enter in the form (project, issue type, summary, description, assignee, priority, labels and any additional fields); and a User-Agent header identifying the extension and its version.
privacyNoticeText.description: Privacy notice listing which user data is sent to the configured Jira server

releaseNotesTitle.description: Title of the release notes page

releaseNotesBack.description: Back button label on the release notes page
